notable_pick_trades: Traded C Garrett Bradbury to CHI for 2027 5th-rounder. Extra 4th (CHI via KC trade). Extra 6ths from KC (Joshua Uche trade), MIN, PIT (Kyle Dugger trade), SF (Keion White trade).
roster_context: Coming off a stunning 14-3 season and Super Bowl LX loss to Seattle under first-year HC Mike Vrabel. Drake Maye is the franchise QB (MVP runner-up). Team made aggressive FA moves but still has clear holes at EDGE and OT. Window is open — this is a contender looking for complementary pieces, not a rebuild.

needs.explanation: The Patriots' most glaring need despite signing Dre'Mont Jones. New England ranked 26th in sacks (35), 26th in pressures, and 29th in hurries in 2025. Lost K'Lavon Chaisson (7.5 sacks) to Washington and released Anfernee Jennings. Harold Landry turns 30 and missed time with a knee injury late in the season, producing zero sacks in three playoff games. Jones helps but the depth behind Landry/Jones is Elijah Ponder, Bradyn Swinson, and Jesse Luketa — all unproven. Eliot Wolf himself noted edge rusher as a position of strength in this draft class, signaling the team's intentions.
needs.depth: Elijah Ponder, Bradyn Swinson, Jesse Luketa — very thin behind starters
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (Pick 31) — strong likelihood given depth of EDGE class

needs.explanation: Morgan Moses is 34 years old and while he played all 21 games effectively in 2025, he is not a long-term answer at RT. Maye was sacked 21 times in four playoff games and was under pressure on 38.7% of playoff dropbacks. Backup OT Vederian Lowe signed with SF, leaving virtually no depth behind Moses (only Marcus Bryant, a raw 7th-round pick). Will Campbell is entrenched at LT but had a shaky postseason that included an MCL injury. Need an heir to Moses at RT who can develop behind him for a year.
needs.current_starter: Will Campbell (LT, 2025 1st-round pick), Morgan Moses (RT, age 34)
needs.depth: Marcus Bryant (raw 7th-rounder) — Vederian Lowe departed to SF

needs.explanation: Released Stefon Diggs ($26M cap hit was untenable) and signed Romeo Doubs (4yr/$80M) as the replacement. Doubs is a solid WR2-level player but not a true WR1 who commands defensive attention the way Diggs did. Kayshon Boutte and Mack Hollins (33, final year) are complementary pieces. Boutte and Douglas are in contract years. The Super Bowl exposed the lack of a true alpha receiver — Diggs had just 101 yards in the entire playoffs. Team has explored an A.J. Brown trade and could still draft a WR. Kyle Williams (3rd-round pick) showed flashes but is unproven.
needs.depth: Mack Hollins (33, final year), DeMario Douglas, Kyle Williams, Efton Chism III

needs.explanation: Released Jahlani Tavai, and Jack Gibbens (10 starts, 90 tackles in 2025) remains unsigned as an RFA. Spillane and Elliss are the only two LBs signed through 2027, making long-term depth a concern. Behind them, the group is mostly special teamers (Marte Mapu, KJ Britt, Chad Muma). Spillane had an ankle injury late in 2025. Need a dynamic playmaker next to Spillane who can be a long-term piece.
needs.depth: Marte Mapu, KJ Britt (signed 1yr), Chad Muma, Otis Reese — mostly ST contributors

needs.explanation: Signed Alijah Vera-Tucker (3yr/$42M) to play LG, moving Jared Wilson from LG to C (replacing traded Garrett Bradbury). Wilson's PFF pass-block grade was a poor 42.8 at guard — his transition to center is unproven. Mike Onwenu (RG) bounced back with a solid season but is 29, in his final contract year, and has a $25M cap hit. Interior OL depth is a concern, and Wilson's development at center is a major question mark after the playoff struggles.
needs.current_starter: Alijah Vera-Tucker (LG, signed 3yr/$42M), Jared Wilson (C, transitioning), Mike Onwenu (RG)
needs.depth: Ben Brown (backup IOL), limited depth overall

needs.explanation: Milton Williams and Christian Barmore form a solid starting duo, but lost run-stuffing DT Khyiris Tonga to KC in free agency. Tonga was a key contributor as a run-stuffer and even moonlighted as a fullback. Depth behind the starters (Cory Durden, Joshua Farmer, Leonard Taylor III) is unproven. Barmore is a talent but has had health scares (autoimmune condition in 2024). Could use another rotational piece.
needs.depth: Cory Durden, Joshua Farmer (4th-round pick), Leonard Taylor III, Eric Gregory

needs.explanation: Signed Kevin Byard (1yr/$9M) to replace departed Jaylinn Hawkins. Byard was a first-team All-Pro with 7 INTs in 2025 but is 32 and on a one-year deal — not a long-term solution. Craig Woodson had an excellent rookie season and is the long-term starter. Could use a developmental FS behind Byard for 2027.
needs.current_starter: Kevin Byard (FS, signed 1yr/$9M, age 32), Craig Woodson (SS)
needs.depth: Dell Pettus, Mike Brown (signed 1yr), Brenden Schooler

free_agency_impact: Released WR Stefon Diggs (cap savings from $26.5M hit) — elevated WR need
Signed WR Romeo Doubs (4yr/$80M from GB) — partially fills WR void but not a true WR1
Signed EDGE Dre'Mont Jones (3yr/$39.5M from BAL) — helps but EDGE still top need
Lost EDGE K'Lavon Chaisson to Washington (1yr/$12M) — significant pass-rush loss (7.5 sacks)
Released EDGE Anfernee Jennings — saved $3.8M cap, further thins edge depth
Signed IOL Alijah Vera-Tucker (3yr/$42M from NYJ) — fills LG hole, enables Wilson's move to C
Traded C Garrett Bradbury to Chicago (for 2027 5th) — opens center job for Jared Wilson
Signed S Kevin Byard (1yr/$9M from CHI) — All-Pro replaces departed Jaylinn Hawkins
Lost S Jaylinn Hawkins to Baltimore (2yr/$10M) — Byard signing mitigates this
Lost DT Khyiris Tonga to Kansas City (3yr/$21M) — DL depth takes a hit
Lost OT Vederian Lowe to San Francisco (2yr/$12M) — backup LT depth gone
Lost TE Austin Hooper to Atlanta — signed Julian Hill (3yr/$18M) as replacement
Released LB Jahlani Tavai — LB depth thinner, Gibbens still unsigned as RFA
Released LB Anfernee Jennings — saves cap, EDGE room reshuffled
Signed FB Reggie Gilliam (3yr/$12M from BUF) — new fullback in McDaniels' offense
Signed LB KJ Britt (1yr deal) — ST/LB depth piece
scheme_fit_notes: Under Mike Vrabel, the Patriots run a multiple defense that leans 3-4 with versatile edge rushers. Vrabel values power EDGE players who can set the edge against the run AND rush the passer — pure speed rushers are less ideal. The OL needs to fit Josh McDaniels' offense, which relies on play-action and requires strong run-blocking. OT prospects should be plus run-blockers. At WR, McDaniels values route precision and reliability over raw athleticism. At LB, the scheme needs a three-down player with coverage ability who can also blitz through gaps.
